title: Reduce the amount of consecutive RUN instructions
description: Reduce the amount of consecutive RUN instructionsAnalyze your codeconsistency-conventionalmaintainabilityCode SmellMultiplying the number ofRUNinstructions increases the final image build time and size.Why is this an issue?How can I fix it?More InfoEach time aRUNinstruction is added, a new layer is introduced in the final image.This has a direct impact on the build time and image size. Chaining commands in a singleRUNinstruction using&&will use a single layer, thereby reducing the number of layers in the image.This practice can make Docker images more efficient and easier to manage.Each layer in a Docker image is essentially a change to the image, like a version control system.So, fewer layers mean fewer changes to track, which simplifies management and troubleshooting.ExceptionsIn multi-s
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.
